title: Apple Revenue by Product Line
blurb: Every product line Apple has bet the company on, racing by annual revenue since 2000. Watch the iPod build an empire, hand it to the iPhone in a single fiscal year, and then watch Services quietly become the second-biggest business Apple has ever built. A companion race to One Infinite Fruit, the Apple device archive.

source.name: Apple Inc. annual reports (Form 10-K)
source.note: Fiscal years ending September. Segment definitions shifted over time: early 'iPod' includes music-related revenue; 'Services' broke out of iTunes/software lines in 2013; Wearables reported separately from 2015.

milestones.title: iPod launches
milestones.text: A thousand songs in your pocket. The $399 music player starts as a Mac accessory and becomes the company's identity for a decade.

milestones.title: iPod out-earns the Mac
milestones.text: The accessory overtakes the computer: iPod revenue passes the entire Mac line 22 years after the Macintosh shipped.

milestones.title: iPhone launches
milestones.text: Steve Jobs introduces a widescreen iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator — one device that will dwarf every other line.

milestones.title: iPhone passes iPod
milestones.text: Two years in, the iPhone out-earns the product it grew out of. The iPod never leads again and is discontinued in 2022.

milestones.title: iPad's fastest-ever ramp
milestones.text: Two years after launch the iPad books $30.9B — briefly Apple's clear #2 and faster out of the gate than the iPhone itself.

milestones.title: Services takes second place
milestones.text: The App Store, iCloud and Music quietly out-earn every hardware line except the iPhone — a subscription empire built on the install base.

milestones.title: The $191B iPhone year
milestones.text: The 5G-era iPhone 12 cycle produces more revenue in one year than Apple's entire company made in its first 30 combined.
